blocks.text: A short status and one request. The baseline is coming together. We have three years of your billing and contract history and the CRM export of account status, and Mary Parker has started reconciling the two so that we count a lost customer the same way your finance and sales teams do. Nothing to report as a finding yet; we are still squaring the two records against each other.

The one gap is the reasons-for-loss field in the CRM. It is filled in for recent accounts and blank for most of the older ones, which is exactly the stretch we need to understand churn on {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}. If anyone on your side kept renewal notes, cancellation letters, or save-desk logs for the accounts that left before this year, those would let us fill it. Even a partial set helps.

Nothing else needed from you today. Once the reconciliation settles, Mary Parker and I will send a short read on what the baseline shows before we walk you through it. The data your team at Carroll, Merritt and Williams has sent so far has come in clean and well labeled, which keeps us on schedule.
